ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002490 covers hamburger as a food item; this record is a double-patty spicy tomato variant — closest_parent match used as no exact double-patty spicy burger code exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers hamburger; double-patty and spicy tomato sauce specifics are not separately coded — closest_parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT food concept found for spicy tomato double beef burger; null applied rather than invent a code.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard double-beef burger weighing approximately 220 g, comprising two beef patties, a bun, spicy tomato sauce, and garnishes.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Double Beef Burger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Spicy Tomato Double Beef Burger (cr_id G00010119) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for beef burger patty and bun composite (see notes).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on white-bread bun composite burger
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Spicy Tomato Double Beef Burger. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a standard beef hamburger on a white bun (GI ~66, medium category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (66) × available carbohydrate per serving (29.57 g/100g × 220 g serving = 65.05 g) ÷ 100 = 42.9, rounded to 43.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 487 mg/100g with low potassium offset (197 mg/100g) and low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger; a 220 g serving delivers approximately 1,071 mg sodium (nearly half the 2,300 mg daily limit), and the low potassium-to-sodium ratio provides insufficient cardiovascular offset — request reduced sauce and avoid adding condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined-carbohydrate load (29.57 g/100g, GI ~66) with low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g) and saturated fat from double beef patties worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit this burger due to its medium-to-high glycaemic impact (GI 66, GL ~43 per serving), low fibre content that accelerates glucose absorption, and saturated fat from double beef patties that exacerbates insulin resistance — if consumed, pair with a high-fibre side and choose a wholegrain bun where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g/100g), trans fat (0.3 g/100g), sodium (487 mg/100g), and cholesterol (29 mg/100g) from double beef patties and processed bun
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This burger is high concern for cardiovascular disease due to its combined saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium load — a 220 g serving contributes approximately 8.4 g saturated fat and 1,071 mg sodium, both of which elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure; consumption should be infrequent and portion-controlled.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High arachidonic acid load from double beef patties, trans fat (0.3 g/100g), and pro-inflammatory nightshade ingredients (tomato, chili) in spicy sauce
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit this burger; the double beef patties significantly increase omega-6 arachidonic acid intake, trans fat at 0.3 g/100g is pro-inflammatory at any dose, and nightshade-derived tomato and chili in the sauce may trigger joint flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Double Beef Burger is a Western-style fast-food burger popular in Singapore, featuring two grilled beef patties stacked in a soft white bun and dressed with a rich, tangy, chili-spiked tomato sauce. The double-patty construction delivers a dense, juicy texture with a pronounced savoury-spicy flavour profile. Per 100 g, it provides 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, and 29.57 g carbohydrate, with 487 mg sodium and 1.8 g dietary fibre. The refined bun contributes the majority of the carbohydrate load, and the medium glycaemic index (~66) means blood glucose rises moderately but substantially per serving. Clinically, this burger is high concern for hypertension (sodium 487 mg/100g, low potassium offset), type 2 diabetes (medium-high GI, low fibre, saturated fat worsening insulin resistance), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat ~3.8 g/100g, trans fat 0.3 g/100g, cholesterol 29 mg/100g). It is also pro-inflammatory for rheumatoid arthritis due to arachidonic acid from double beef and nightshade sauce ingredients. It is not vegetarian, not vegan, and contains gluten and dairy allergens. Suitable for occasional consumption only; portion control and sauce reduction are recommended for health-conscious consumers.
common_user_queries: Is the spicy tomato double beef burger healthy?
How many calories in a double beef burger?
Can diabetics eat a double beef burger?
Is this burger high in sodium?
What is the GI of a beef burger?
Is this burger suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo Singapore database (cr_id G00010119, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy beef burger composite as HPB did not report this field. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for a standard beef hamburger on white bun.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this item, and no sufficiently close USDA SR Legacy composite was available to reliably disaggregate MUFA/PUFA for this specific double-patty spicy tomato formulation. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported by HPB for this entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) is not available in the HPB Foodo source for this record. trans_fat_g of 0.3 g/100g is carried forward from the input health_suitability clinical notes referencing probable trans fat in the double-patty formulation; this is an estimate and should be verified against a certified lab analysis. saturated_fat_g of 3.8 g/100g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 'Beef, ground, 80% lean meat / 20% fat, patty, cooked, pan-broiled' combined with a standard white hamburger bun composite, adapted proportionally for a double-patty 220 g burger; this is an adapted estimate. Halal status is not confirmed from the HPB record and is set to false pending verification.
